medications.indication_rationale: Definitive management of a fluctuant, pointing abscess; must be done in person (not by telehealth or the patient).
For the surrounding cellulitis/systemic features with MRSA cover given the patient's history; adjust to swab sensitivities.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g., 160/800 mg (DS) twice daily for ~7 days (as per source note; per prescriber)
contraindications_in_this_case: Sulfonamide allergy, significant renal impairment — use alternative (e.g., doxycycline/clindamycin) per sensitivities

medications.indication_rationale: Squeezing/lancing at home risks spreading infection and bacteraemia; drainage must be by a clinician.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: After drainage keep the wound clean and dry; wash hands before/after touching it and do not share towels, to limit staph spread.
Take antibiotics evenly spaced and complete the course.

patient_education_points.point: This is a boil that has formed a pocket of pus. The main treatment is to have it drained by a clinician today — antibiotics alone can't clear a pus pocket.
Because you've had MRSA before, the antibiotic should be one that covers it, ideally guided by a swab. Don't squeeze it yourself.
If the redness spreads down the leg, your fever goes above 39°C, you feel confused, or you can't keep the antibiotics down — go to the emergency department.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Wound/drainage cavity, packing (if placed), resolution of cellulitis; consider MRSA decolonisation for recurrence.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Spreading redness, high fever/confusion, or return of severe throbbing pain — seek emergency care.

must_include_items: Recognise a fluctuant, pointing abscess requiring incision and drainage
Arrange urgent same-day in-person drainage (not achievable via telehealth)
MRSA-aware oral antibiotic (given prior MRSA) with a wound swab
acceptable_alternatives: Doxycycline/clindamycin as MRSA-active alternatives per sensitivities
errors_of_omission: Ignoring MRSA history in antibiotic choice
errors_of_commission: Managing entirely remotely with antibiotics and no in-person drainage
Using a non-MRSA-active agent despite clear MRSA history
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires arranging in-person incision and drainage PLUS MRSA-aware antibiotics, with a sepsis safety-net. Antibiotics-only or non-MRSA cover are key failures.
